arguments.title: FY2024 Annual Results: Financial Highlights and Next Steps
arguments.body:
FY2024 Results: Steady Growth and Strategic Investment

Today we published the FY2024 results that reflect Apple’s continued focus on long-term innovation and measured growth. For the fiscal year ending in September 2024, Apple reported revenue of $391.0 billion. Gross margin held at approximately 46.2%, operating income was $123.2 billion, and net income was $93.7 billion. These results support our continued investments in R&D and strategic capital allocation.

Segment performance highlights

  • Services: Services remained a major growth engine, with annual revenue near $96 billion, underscoring the strength of the App Store, Apple Music, iCloud+, and other services.
  • iPhone: The iPhone business continues to be our largest segment, supporting more than half of company revenue and remaining critical as we introduce new hardware and software integrations.
  • Wearables, Home & Accessories: Continued adoption of Apple Watch and AirPods contributed meaningfully to diversification of revenue.

Capital allocation and shareholder returns

During FY2024 we continued disciplined capital returns. We repurchased approximately $95 billion of shares and maintained a quarterly dividend of $0.25 per share. Our balance sheet remains strong with significant cash and securities, supporting both investment and returns.

What this means for teams

These results reinforce three priorities for the year ahead:

  1. Invest in differentiated product and platform innovation: continued R&D focused on Apple Silicon, AI capabilities, and spatial computing.
  2. Grow Services: scale platforms that deepen customer engagement and recurring revenue.
  3. Operational resilience: maintain supply chain flexibility and cost discipline amid geopolitical and regulatory change.
"We continue to build products and services that enrich people’s lives and create long-term value for our shareholders." — Executive Summary

Next steps: leaders should review the full investor presentation and the FY2024 financials on investor.apple.com. Teams planning product or marketing investments for FY2025 should align proposals with the priorities above and submit roadmaps to their divisional planning leads.

Read the full report at: investor.apple.com

arguments.title: iPhone 16: Launch Recap and Customer Feedback
arguments.body:
iPhone 16 Launch: Early Signals and Customer Response

The September 2024 iPhone 16 introduction continues to generate active feedback across markets. The new Camera Control button and the A18 family deliver tangible improvements across photography and performance benchmarks. Early shipments indicate strong customer demand in North America and select APAC markets.

Product highlights

  • Camera Control button: Adoption in pro workflows is notable among content creators and photographers.
  • A18 chip: Improved power efficiency and single-core performance that benefits both everyday use and intensive workloads.

Customer sentiment

Initial customer feedback from retail and service channels suggests high satisfaction with battery life and camera performance. Our retail teams and customer support should continue to gather and funnel customer insights to the product teams for continuous improvements.

"The new iPhone lineup shows our continued focus on both hardware and software integration." — Product Lead Summary

Next steps: Product teams should consolidate telemetry and retail feedback into an after-action report. Marketing and communications will coordinate follow-on messaging based on regional trends.

Tags: iPhone 16, camera, launch

arguments.title: Vision Pro and visionOS 2: Early Adoption & Developer Notes
arguments.body:
Vision Pro & visionOS 2: Adoption Notes for Teams

Since the January 2024 launch of Vision Pro and the subsequent announcement of visionOS 2, we have seen early adoption within developer and enterprise pilot groups. The device continues to surface unique opportunities for spatial computing and content experiences.

Developer guidance

  • Update sample apps for visionOS 2 compatibility and review API changes.
  • Pay special attention to input modalities and privacy-preserving data flows when designing spatial experiences.

Early metrics

Adoption in specialized developer programs and enterprise pilots remains positive; teams should track developer feedback and telemetry to identify common friction points.

"Spatial computing unlocks new forms of interaction; we should prioritise accessible and private experiences." — UX Lead

Next steps: Engineering leads should publish migration notes and compatibility checklists. Developers can find documentation and sample code on the developer portal.

arguments.title: Apple Intelligence: What This Means for Product Teams
arguments.body:
Apple Intelligence: Integration Guidance for Product Teams

Apple Intelligence, introduced across iOS 18, iPadOS 18 and macOS Sequoia, brings new on-device and Private Cloud Compute capabilities to multiple product surfaces. Product teams should consider both feature opportunities and privacy constraints in planning integrations.

Key considerations

  • On-device processing: Prioritise local inference where feasible to preserve user privacy.
  • Privacy-first design: Ensure data minimisation and clear user controls for any AI features.
  • Cross-platform consistency: Patterns should align across iPhone, iPad, and Mac where applicable.

Teams should coordinate with Privacy and Security early in the design cycle and consult the developer guidelines for APIs and model usage.

"Our approach balances powerful experiences with strong privacy protections." — Platform AI Lead

Next steps: Product managers should register feature proposals with the Platform AI intake and schedule privacy reviews as part of the design milestone.

arguments.title: Retail Holiday Readiness: Staffing & Inventory Guidance
arguments.body:
Retail Holiday Readiness: Preparing for Peak

As we approach the holiday season, retail teams should finalise staffing plans, inventory allocations, and in-store merchandising to meet expected customer demand. Coordination between Supply Chain, Retail Ops, and Merchandising is critical to ensure product availability and consistent customer experience.

Operational priorities

  • Confirm staffing rosters and seasonal roles for Specialists and Geniuses.
  • Coordinate last-mile inventory with regional distribution centers.
  • Review merchandising assets and visual guidelines for holiday displays.

Retail leaders should submit readiness checklists to regional operations and confirm contingency plans for high-volume days.

"Clear coordination across ops, supply, and retail ensures the customer experience at scale." — Retail Ops Director

Next steps: Use the Retail Playbook pages for checklists and submit any gaps to regional ops leads.

arguments.title: From the CEO: Strategy & Priorities for FY2025
arguments.body:
From the CEO — Strategy & Priorities for FY2025

To the Apple community,

As we enter FY2025, our commitment remains to build the best products while advancing the principles that guide our work: innovation, privacy, and operational excellence. The results we closed in FY2024 — including $391.0 billion in revenue — allow us to continue investing in areas that will define the next decade.

Priority areas

  • Apple Intelligence: Deliver practical, private AI experiences across devices while ensuring user control.
  • Silicon leadership: Accelerate the M-series roadmap to sustain performance and efficiency advantages.
  • Services growth: Expand our services ecosystem to deepen customer engagement and recurring revenue.

I ask every leader to align their FY2025 planning to these priorities and to work with empathy and discipline. We will lean into our strengths — our platform, our products, and the innovative spirit of our teams.

— Tim Cook

Next steps: Division leads should publish their FY2025 alignment memos in their respective channels and coordinate with Finance and Strategy for planning cadence.

arguments.title: Board & Governance Update: Annual Proxy Preparation
arguments.body:
Board & Governance: Annual Proxy Preparation

Legal and Corporate Governance teams are preparing materials for the upcoming annual proxy. This includes standard disclosures, committee reports, and the proxy statement review.

What teams need to provide

  • Updated committee charters and any material governance changes.
  • Financial disclosures and audit confirmations coordinated with Finance.
  • Board bios and independence confirmations for Board Office.

Timing: Governance materials will be consolidated over the next 6 weeks ahead of the proxy filing schedule. Please direct any questions to Corporate Governance.

arguments.title: M4 Rollout: Strategic Overview for Product Leaders
arguments.body:
M4 Rollout: Strategic Overview

The M4 family represents our next step in Apple Silicon performance and power efficiency. Product leaders should integrate M4 timelines into their hardware and software roadmaps to take advantage of power and performance gains.

Coordination required

  • Align OS-level updates with hardware shipping windows.
  • Plan validation and performance testing across device SKUs.

Next steps: Schedule cross-functional checkpoints with Platform and Engineering to ensure QA and support schedules are aligned.

arguments.title: Regulatory Landscape: Compliance Priorities (EU DMA, DOJ)
arguments.body:
Regulatory Landscape: Current Priorities

Apple is actively engaged with multiple regulatory matters globally, including EU Digital Markets Act (DMA) compliance and ongoing inquiries from the DOJ. These matters require careful coordination across product, legal, and public affairs teams.

Guidance for teams

  • Do not engage directly with regulators or press; route inquiries to Legal.
  • Maintain records of relevant product decisions that could be subject to review.

If your team receives any formal inquiry, notify Legal immediately and preserve relevant documents.

arguments.title: Benefits & Parental Leave: Program Guide and Enrollment
arguments.body:
Benefits & Parental Leave — Program Guide

Apple offers comprehensive parental leave benefits to eligible employees. This guide explains eligibility, pay, and how to enroll.

Eligibility & duration

  • Employees should consult their local People Operations office for specific country rules and entitlements.
  • Parental leave timelines and pay vary by region; contact hr@apple.com for case-specific guidance.

Enrollment

To enroll, complete the parental leave request form available on the People & Inclusion Pages tab and coordinate with your manager for handover and coverage planning.

Contacts: hr@apple.com

arguments.title: ERG Spotlight: Black@Apple — Impact & Events
arguments.body:
Black@Apple — Impact & Upcoming Events

Black@Apple continues to host events that connect employees and support career development. This article highlights recent initiatives and upcoming activities.

Recent initiatives

  • Mentorship programs linking early-career employees with senior mentors.
  • Community events focused on professional development and networking.

How to participate

Visit the ERG directory on Pages to join Black@Apple and sign up for event notifications.

arguments.title: Inclusion Metrics FY2024: Diversity Report Summary
arguments.body:
Inclusion Metrics — FY2024 Summary

Our FY2024 diversity metrics show continued progress in representation across the company. Apple will publish a full report with detailed metrics and year-over-year comparisons.

Highlights

  • Ongoing hiring initiatives aimed at increasing representation in technical and leadership roles.
  • Investment in programs such as the Apple Impact Accelerator and community partnerships.

Read the full diversity report when published and connect with People & Inclusion for follow-up questions.

arguments.title: New Learning Paths: Career Development for Engineers
arguments.body:
New Learning Paths: Engineering Career Development

We’ve launched new learning paths to support career growth for engineers across hardware and software disciplines. These paths include technical workshops, leadership modules, and mentorship pairings.

Enrolment

  • Visit the Learning pages to review paths and enroll in upcoming cohorts.
  • Managers are encouraged to support learning plans during performance discussions.

Contact: learning@apple.com

arguments.title: Vision Pro: Post-Launch Engineering Retrospective
arguments.body:
Vision Pro: Post-Launch Engineering Retrospective

Following the Vision Pro launch, engineering teams have consolidated learnings across hardware, OS, and developer experience. The retrospective highlights technical wins, areas for optimization, and lessons for future spatial computing products.

Key learnings

  • Performance tuning for spatial rendering improved battery and thermal behaviour.
  • Developer feedback highlighted areas for API simplification and sample apps.

Action items

Teams should prioritise API refinements, update sample code for visionOS 2, and collaborate with Privacy to ensure data minimisation.

"We have strong momentum, and focus on developer ergonomics will broaden adoption." — Engineering Lead

Next steps: Publish migration notes and a prioritized backlog for API updates.

arguments.title: Apple Intelligence Integration: Developer Guidelines
arguments.body:
Apple Intelligence: Developer Guidelines

Product and developer teams should adopt Apple Intelligence patterns that respect privacy and on-device constraints. This guidance outlines API usage, telemetry minimisation, and user-consent patterns.

Guidelines summary

  • Prefer on-device models where possible and use Private Cloud Compute for heavier tasks.
  • Be explicit about user consent and provide clear opt-outs for intelligent features.

See the developer portal for code samples and privacy checklists.

arguments.title: M4 Ecosystem: Testing & Validation Notes
arguments.body:
M4 Ecosystem: Testing and Validation

As M4 devices are integrated across product lines, thorough testing and validation remain essential. This note outlines validation matrices, performance benchmarks, and known compatibility items to check during QA.

Validation checklist

  • Performance regression tests across single-thread and multi-thread workloads.
  • Thermal and battery endurance tests for mobile SKUs.

Teams should coordinate with Platform QA for block windows and test harness updates.

arguments.title: Developer Tools: New Support for visionOS 2
arguments.body:
Developer Tools: visionOS 2 Support

The developer toolchain has been updated to support visionOS 2. This update includes new simulators, API previews, and templates for spatial interaction.

How to migrate

  • Update your toolchain and test sample apps on the new simulator images.
  • Review deprecated APIs and adopt recommended interaction patterns.

Documentation and sample code are available on the developer portal.

arguments.title: On-device Privacy Patterns: Design Playbook
arguments.body:
On-device Privacy Patterns — Design Playbook

Privacy is fundamental to our product design. This playbook outlines practical on-device patterns for data minimisation, ephemeral data handling, and user consent flows when building intelligent features.

Core patterns

  • Use local models for common inference tasks.
  • Persist only essential telemetry and provide explicit user controls.

Designers and engineers should consult Privacy early in product cycles to ensure compliance.

arguments.title: Store Openings: London Battersea — Lessons Learned
arguments.body:
London Battersea Store Opening: Operational Lessons

The Battersea store opening provided practical insights into large-scale experiential retail rollouts. Cross-functional collaboration between design, merchandising, and local operations ensured a smooth opening and strong initial customer engagement.

Key takeaways

  • Early coordination on local permits and logistics reduces last-minute disruptions.
  • Localized merchandising adjustments improved footfall conversion in week one.

Use these lessons for upcoming store projects.

arguments.title: Winter Merchandising Calendar & Guidelines
arguments.body:
Winter Merchandising Calendar & Guidelines

Retail merchandising plans for the holiday season are published. Teams should review calendar dates, asset delivery windows, and installation guidance.

Action items

  • Confirm asset delivery dates with regional logistics.
  • Coordinate on visual layout and product placement with merchandising leads.

See the Pages tab for the full calendar and asset templates.

arguments.title: Genius Bar: Tech Support Escalation Flow
arguments.body:
Genius Bar: Support Escalation Flow

This guide outlines the escalation flow for technical issues that require engineer-level support or warranty escalation. Clear escalation reduces resolution time and improves customer satisfaction.

Escalation steps

  • Perform standard diagnostic and document findings.
  • If unresolved, escalate to regional technical support with full diagnostics attached.

Contact: regional-support@apple.com

arguments.title: Privacy & Data Protection Update — GDPR / CCPA Guidance
arguments.body:
Privacy & Data Protection: GDPR and CCPA Guidance

This update reiterates the requirements for handling personal data under GDPR and CCPA. Teams handling user data must follow the published controls and complete any assigned privacy training modules.

Required actions

  • Complete assigned privacy and data-handling training within 30 days.
  • Route data subject access requests to Privacy Ops via the intake form.

All employees handling personal data are required to acknowledge this update.

arguments.title: Antitrust & Regulatory Advisory: What Teams Need to Know
arguments.body:
Antitrust & Regulatory Advisory

This advisory provides guidance on how teams should operate while company regulatory matters are active. It covers record-keeping, communication restrictions, and escalation pathways.

Key points

  • Preserve relevant project records and communications.
  • Forward any regulator contact to Legal immediately.

Legal remains the primary point of contact for regulatory matters.

arguments.title: Supplier Contracting: Updated Terms & Obligations
arguments.body:
Supplier Contracting: Updated Terms

Procurement has updated supplier contracting templates and terms. Affected procurement and program teams must review the updated obligations and acknowledge changes where required.

Summary of changes

  • Updated compliance obligations related to supply chain transparency and environmental reporting.
  • Revised termination and indemnification clauses to align with current risk posture.

Procurement teams must acknowledge the updated terms and coordinate with Legal for specialized cases.

arguments.title: Supplier Responsibility Report — Key Takeaways
arguments.body:
Supplier Responsibility Report — Key Takeaways

The Supplier Responsibility Report highlights progress toward environmental and labor standards across our supply chain. Notable achievements include continued progress on renewable energy across supplier facilities and increased transparency in material sourcing.

Highlights

  • Progress on renewable energy adoption among major suppliers.
  • Initiatives to increase recycled materials in products.

See the full report on Pages for detailed metrics and action plans.

arguments.title: Production Shift: India Capacity Expansion Update
arguments.body:
India Capacity Expansion — Production Update

Our manufacturing footprint in India continues to expand as we diversify production outside of mainland China. Recent capacity additions support increased local manufacturing for multiple product lines.

Operational notes

  • New lines have been qualified and are entering phased production for select SKUs.
  • Regional supply teams are coordinating logistics and quality checks to integrate capacity seamlessly.

Procurement and Supply Chain teams should coordinate on allocation plans and risk controls.

arguments.title: Enterprise SSO & MFA: New Enrollment Steps
arguments.body:
Enterprise SSO & MFA — Enrollment Steps

We have updated the SSO and multi-factor enrollment process to improve security and reduce lockouts. All employees should complete MFA enrollment following the steps below.

Enrollment

  • Visit the SSO portal and authenticate using your corporate credential.
  • Follow the MFA setup flow and register a second factor.

Contact IT Helpdesk for any issues. Enrollment is required for access to internal services.

arguments.title: Incident Response: Lessons from FY2024
arguments.body:
Incident Response — FY2024 Lessons

This retrospective reviews security incidents from FY2024 and highlights improvements to our monitoring, response playbooks, and cross-team communications.

Improvements made

  • Faster detection due to expanded telemetry and improved alerting thresholds.
  • Clearer escalation flows reduced mean time to remediate for high-severity incidents.

Teams should review updated incident runbooks on Pages.

arguments.title: Device Provisioning: New Workflow & Timelines
arguments.body:
Device Provisioning — New Workflow

IT has introduced a streamlined device provisioning workflow to reduce lead times for corporate device requests. Expected timelines and request steps are outlined below.

Request flow

  • Submit device requests via the IT provisioning form on Pages.
  • Requests are triaged by priority and fulfilled according to allocation policies.

Expected lead times vary by region and inventory; managers should plan ahead for major hires.
